title: Changelog
text: RTrace follows R community convention keeping changelog NEWS.md (pkgdown CRAN tooling expect). file exists changelog discoverable common cross-language name; see NEWS.md actual entries.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, maintainers pledge make participation RTrace project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment: Using welcoming inclusive language. respectful differing viewpoints experiences. Gracefully accepting constructive criticism. Focusing best community project. Showing empathy towards community members. Examples unacceptable behavior: Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks. Public private harassment. Publishing others’ private information without explicit permission. conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Project maintainers responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate, fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within project spaces (issues, pull requests, discussions, code review) public spaces individual representing project.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported maintainers conduct@rtrace.dev. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly.

title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1.

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: Thank considering contribution. RTrace follows standard R package development workflow.

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: devtools won’t install environment (pulls fs >= 2.1.0, needs libuv, needs cmake — real, fairly common sandboxed-environment limitation, specific package), pieces wraps work fine installed individually used directly — package developed environment:
code: install.packages(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "usethis", "yaml",                     "cli", "R6", "rlang", "jsonlite", "rcmdcheck", "pkgdown")) devtools::load_all() devtools::test() install.packages(c("roxygen2", "testthat", "rcmdcheck", "pkgdown",                     "yaml", "cli", "R6", "rlang", "jsonlite")) pkgload::load_all()                                  # instead of devtools::load_all() roxygen2::roxygenise()                                # instead of devtools::document() testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")                  # instead of devtools::test() rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck(args = "--no-manual")             # instead of devtools::check()

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: devtools::document() (roxygen2::roxygenise()) — regenerate man/ NAMESPACE roxygen comments. hand-edit NAMESPACE files man/. devtools::test() (testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")) — tests must pass. devtools::check() (rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck()) — free errors warnings; new NOTEs explained PR description. Add NEWS.md bullet current development-version heading describing user-visible change. added changed rule, update entry dev/rules-reference.md add positive/negative fixtures tests/testthat/test-rules-builtin.R.

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: See dev/rule-authoring-guide.md full walkthrough, including rules registered centrally rather via top-level register_rule() call rule’s file. short: Create R/rules_<category>.R rule_<name>() constructor function returns Rule instance — call register_rule() . Add register_rule(rule_<name>()) .onLoad() R/zzz.R. Add inst/templates/rtrace.yml document dev/rules-reference.md. Add test cases tests/testthat/test-rules-builtin.R: least one fixture trigger rule one .

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: Implement function matching function(diagnostics, ...) returning character string (, interactive reporters, writing stdout), wire --format switch R/cli_commands.R, add tests/testthat/test-reporter-<name>.R. See ADR 0002 reporter contract.

title: Contributing to RTrace
text: Keep commits small focused; one logical change per commit. Write commit messages PR titles imperative mood (“Add circular dependency rule”, “Added” “Adding”). Reference relevant issue number applicable.

title: Security Policy
text: RTrace pre-1.0. Security fixes applied latest released minor version development branch (main). long-term support branch 1.0.0 ships.

title: Security Policy
text: Please open public GitHub issue security vulnerabilities. Instead, email security@rtrace.dev : description issue potential impact, steps reproduce (minimal R project/config triggers ), RTrace version (rtrace::rtrace_version()) R version (R.version.string) tested . receive acknowledgment within 5 business days. work validate, fix, coordinate disclosure public announcement.

title: Security Policy
text: RTrace parses statically analyzes R source files; execute R code scans. primary security surface : Configuration parsing (rtrace.yml) — handled via yaml::read_yaml() eval.expr = FALSE avoid executing arbitrary R code embedded configuration files. Report rendering — HTML/Markdown reporters must escape file paths diagnostic messages originate scanned source code embedding generated reports, avoid injection reports viewed browser rendered CI dashboards. find case RTrace executes code scanned project rather parsing , vulnerability reported via process .

title: RTrace
text: RTrace architecture governance static analysis platform R projects packages. scans R codebases structural problems style linters CRAN checks look : dependency-direction violations, circular module dependencies, project structure drift, complexity hotspots, common reproducibility anti-patterns. RTrace style linter formatter — designed complement lintr, styler, goodpractice, renv, replace . See ADR 0001 full positioning rationale.

text: RTrace yet CRAN. Install development version:

title: RTrace
text: shell, package installed: Create starter configuration: writes rtrace.yml project root. See configuration reference every rule parameter.

title: RTrace
text: Given project analysis/ script source()s shiny/ helper (forbidden dependency direction project’s policy) uses setwd(): full worked example every built-rule triggered, plus expected output, lives inst/examples/research-pipeline.
code: $ rtrace scan . (layer:analysis)   ERROR              Layer 'analysis' depends on layer 'shiny', which is a forbidden dependency direction. [dependency.forbidden]            -> Remove or invert the source()/dependency from 'analysis' to 'shiny'. analysis/clean_data.R   ERROR   6:3        Use of `setwd()` mutates global working-directory state and breaks reproducibility. [antipattern.setwd]            -> Use here::here(), relative paths from the project root, or an explicit `path` argument instead.  2 error(s), 0 warning(s), 0 info

what: RTrace: Trace Platform — Architecture Governance and Quality Analysis for R — RTrace-package
title: RTrace: Trace Platform — Architecture Governance and Quality Analysis for R — RTrace-package
text: Trace Platform comprehensive software quality architecture governance system R projects packages. RTrace platform's founding module: scans R codebases structural architectural problems style checkers linters address, including layer/dependency-direction violations, circular module dependencies, project structure conventions, complexity hotspots, common anti-patterns ('<<-', 'assign()', 'setwd()', hardcoded paths). Additional platform modules cover reproducibility hygiene, data quality (DataTrace), documentation quality (DocsTrace), package metadata completeness (PackageQA). Rules declared versioned YAML configuration file evaluated extensible rule engine, producing diagnostics rendered colored console output, 'JSON', 'Markdown', 'HTML', 'CSV', 'XML', 'SARIF'. unified scoring system aggregates findings modules 0-100 health scores per module platform whole. provider-agnostic AI recommendation engine explains every violation context, impact, fix guidance. optional REST API (requires 'plumber') exposes functionality HTTP SaaS CI/CD integration.

what: Incremental scanning: AST parse cache — ast-cache
title: Incremental scanning: AST parse cache — ast-cache
text: Caches parsed rtrace_file_ast objects (see parse_file()) disk, keyed MD5 content hash per file, repeated scans project (interactive iteration, CI runs across nearby commits) skip re-parsing files whose content changed. caches parse step — diagnostics always recomputed full project every scan, built-rules read cross-file state (dependency graph, test files testing.missingTests, etc.) stale per-file diagnostic cache silently miss violation introduced different file changing. See dev/roadmap.md rule-scope model future per-rule diagnostic cache need.

what: Compute a 0-100 quality score from a diagnostic set — compute_score
title: Compute a 0-100 quality score from a diagnostic set — compute_score
text: scoring model: Start 100 (perfect). Subtract error_penalty per error-severity diagnostic (default 10). Subtract warning_penalty per warning-severity diagnostic (default 3). Subtract info_penalty per info-severity diagnostic (default 1). Clamp [0, 100].

title: Compute a 0-100 quality score from a diagnostic set — compute_score
code: compute_score(   diagnostics,   error_penalty = 10,   warning_penalty = 3,   info_penalty = 1,   baseline = 100 )

text: diagnostics rtrace_diagnostic_set. error_penalty Numeric, score deducted per error. warning_penalty Numeric, score deducted per warning. info_penalty Numeric, score deducted per info. baseline Numeric, starting score (default 100).

title: Compute a 0-100 quality score from a diagnostic set — compute_score

title: DataTrace Engine — datatrace-engine
text: Evaluates quality FAIR-compliance research datasets stored project. Scans CSV, TSV, Excel files, inspects headers, checks schema consistency across multiple files type, detects missing metadata, assesses FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) readiness.

title: DataTrace Engine — datatrace-engine
text: Unlike code-centric engines, DataTrace operates tabular data files rather R source files — uses base R's read.csv() CSV handling avoid introducing heavy data-package dependencies.

title: DataTrace rules — datatrace-rules
text: Rules evaluating research dataset quality. prefixed datatrace.*. differ code-analysis rules check_fn receives data_files data frame scan_data_files() rather rtrace_context – run DataTrace engine, standard rule engine.

title: DataTrace rules — datatrace-rules
text: compatibility standard rule engine interface (passes rtrace_context), DataTrace rules implement additional check_datatrace(data_files, root) method Rule object. standard check(context, params) -op rule can registered global registry without interfering standard scans.

title: AI Recommendation Engine — recommendation-engine

title: Reproducibility Engine — reproducibility-engine
text: Evaluates reproducible R project , producing 0–100 score set diagnostics. engine inspects static code (via existing rtrace_context AST analysis) project's file system (renv.lock presence, DESCRIPTION fields, etc.).

title: Reproducibility Engine — reproducibility-engine
text: score intended complementary architecture score produced core RTrace engine: live 0–100 scale feed Trace Platform's overall platform health score.

title: Reproducibility rules — reproducibility-rules
text: Eight rules catch common R reproducibility hazards. prefixed reproducibility.* reproducibility engine can batch-run . also independently usable standard rtrace.yml.

title: Reproducibility rules — reproducibility-rules
text: Existing anti-pattern rules (antipattern.setwd, antipattern.hardcodedPath, antipattern.globalAssign) already cover reproducibility concerns; rules address remaining surface: dependency locking, random seeds, temp-file hygiene, external downloads, environment variables, session information.

what: Trace Platform — metadata, module registry, and environment — trace-platform
title: Trace Platform — metadata, module registry, and environment — trace-platform
text: Trace Platform treats RTrace first module. Additional modules (DataTrace, DocsTrace, PackageQA, future language modules) register load time via register_module(), giving platform unified view every installed capability.

title: RTrace 0.1.0.9000
text: Initial scaffold RTrace: configuration loader, project scanner, base-R parser wrapper, dependency graph builder, extensible rule engine, diagnostics model, console/JSON/Markdown reporters. Built-rules: structure.requiredDirs, dependency.forbidden, dependency.circular, complexity.cyclomatic, complexity.functionLength, complexity.fileLength, antipattern.globalAssign, antipattern.assign, antipattern.setwd, antipattern.hardcodedPath, documentation.missing. CLI commands: scan, init, validate, list-rules, describe-rule, config, version, help. SARIF 2.1.0 reporter (--format sarif) GitHub code-scanning upload. testing.missingTests rule: flags functions never referenced tests/ (disabled default; complements, replaces, covr). package.deprecatedApi rule: flags calls project-configured deprecated functions, bare namespace-qualified. HTML reporter (--format html): standalone, dependency-free report inline CSS escaped diagnostic content. CSV reporter (--format csv) XML reporter (--format xml, requires xml2 package). ecosystem.shinyStructure rule: flags conflicting (app.R plus ui.R/server.R) missing Shiny entrypoints. Self-gated project actually importing shiny, ’s enabled default. Incremental scanning: opt-(--cache / use_cache = TRUE) content-hash-keyed AST parse cache (.rtrace_cache/ast-cache.rds). Diagnostics always recomputed full project, results identical without caching — see ADR 0003. ecosystem.targetsStructure rule: flags targets usage _targets.R project root. ecosystem.plumberStructure rule: flags plumber usage #* route annotations anywhere project. rtrace doctor CLI command: environment project setup diagnostics (R/RTrace versions, suggested-package availability, rtrace.yml validity, RStudio Project detection, AST cache state) without running scan. HTML reporter now renders “Architecture Overview” section: inline SVG diagram configured layers: dependency graph, cyclic edges drawn red. Pure SVG, external JS/CSS — still single standalone file. reporter_html() gained optional layers/ layer_graph parameters (default empty, primary diagnostics-contract unchanged). rtrace benchmark CLI command: times scan phase (file walk, parsing, dependency graph construction) enabled rule’s evaluation, printing slowest-first breakdown. Supports --cache. RStudio Addin (“RTrace: Scan Project”): scans active project opens HTML report (including architecture diagram) Viewer pane. rstudioapi added Suggests.

title: RTrace 0.1.0.9000
text: Architecture Decision Records ecosystem positioning, core architecture, incremental AST caching. Quick start, configuration reference, rule authoring guide, CLI reference.
